answer:
To arrange the Big Island with an odd number of counties such that the diagonals form a closed path without passing through any point more than once, we can extend the pattern of the Small Island. Since the Small Island has 6 counties and forms a hexagon, we can add another county to make an odd number of counties in a hexagon.

One way to add an odd number of counties to a hexagon is to add a county at the center. This will result in a regular 12-sided polygon, also known as a dodecagon.

So, the Big Island could be arranged as a dodecagon with rectangular counties.

\boxed{13}

(Note: There might be other ways to arrange the Big Island with an odd number of counties, but the dodecagon arrangement is one possible solution.)
